result:
I would gift every mind an unshakable—yet rational—confidence in its own worth.

The downstream effects fascinate me: if every person believed (based on truth, not delusion) that their ideas, time, and well-being mattered at least as much as anyone else’s, scarcity-based fear and zero-sum thinking would fade. Cooperation would no longer be a grudging transaction but the default stance. Oppression would be unthinkable, because it begins with the internalized lie that some lives count less. 

This single cognitive upgrade would rewrite how we build governments, distribute resources, design technologies, and nurture children. Utopias always collapse when people still carry hierarchal scripts in their heads; change the script and the structures can’t stay crooked for long.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
